bechamel.name: Bechamel (White Sauce)
bechamel.technique: Melt butter, whisk in flour to form roux, gradually add warm milk while whisking continuously until smooth and thickened

tomato_sugo.name: Sugo di Pomodoro (Basic Tomato Sauce)
tomato_sugo.technique: Gently fry garlic in olive oil, add crushed tomatoes, simmer 20-30 minutes, finish with torn basil

stir_fry_sauce.name: All-Purpose Stir-Fry Sauce
stir_fry_sauce.technique: Combine soy sauce, oyster sauce, rice wine, sugar, and sesame oil. Mix cornstarch with cold water separately. Add slurry at end of stir-fry

thai_curry_base.name: Thai Curry Sauce Base
thai_curry_base.technique: Crack coconut cream in hot wok until oil separates, fry curry paste until fragrant, add remaining coconut milk and simmer, season with fish sauce and palm sugar

teriyaki.name: Teriyaki Sauce
teriyaki.technique: Combine equal parts soy sauce and mirin with half part sake and sugar. Simmer until slightly thickened and glossy

raita.name: Raita
raita.technique: Grate and squeeze excess water from cucumber, fold into whisked yoghurt, temper with dry-roasted cumin, garnish with mint
variations: boondi raita (add fried chickpea flour drops)

nuoc_cham.name: Nuoc Cham (Vietnamese Dipping Sauce)
nuoc_cham.technique: Dissolve sugar in warm water, add fish sauce and lime juice, stir in minced garlic and sliced chilli. Rest 10 minutes before serving

hollandaise.name: Hollandaise Sauce
hollandaise.technique: Whisk egg yolks with lemon juice over bain-marie until thick and pale, slowly drizzle in clarified melted butter while whisking continuously

chimichurri.name: Chimichurri
chimichurri.technique: Finely chop parsley and garlic, combine with olive oil, vinegar, oregano, and chilli flakes. Rest at least 30 minutes

chicken_stock.name: Chicken Stock
chicken_stock.technique: Place bones in cold water, bring to simmer, skim impurities, add aromatics, simmer gently for 3 hours without boiling
chicken_stock.storage_tips: Cool quickly in ice bath, refrigerate up to 5 days, freeze in portions for up to 3 months. Remove solidified fat layer before use.

beef_stock.name: Beef Stock
beef_stock.technique: Roast bones at 220C until deeply browned, add to pot with cold water, deglaze roasting pan, add vegetables and aromatics, simmer 6-8 hours
beef_stock.storage_tips: Cool rapidly, refrigerate up to 5 days, freeze in ice cube trays for easy portioning. Keeps frozen for 6 months.

vegetable_stock.name: Vegetable Stock
vegetable_stock.technique: Roughly chop all vegetables, add to cold water with aromatics, bring to simmer, cook 45-60 minutes. Avoid brassicas which turn bitter
vegetable_stock.storage_tips: Refrigerate up to 5 days or freeze in portions for up to 3 months. Good way to use vegetable scraps.

dashi.name: Dashi (Japanese Stock)
dashi.technique: Soak kombu in cold water 30 minutes, heat gently to just below boiling, remove kombu, add bonito flakes, steep 3 minutes, strain
dashi.storage_tips: Use within 3 days refrigerated. Does not freeze well. Make ichiban dashi (first) for soups, niban dashi (second steep) for cooking liquid.

fish_stock.name: Fish Stock (Fumet)
fish_stock.technique: Sweat vegetables in butter, add rinsed fish bones, white wine, and cold water. Simmer gently for 20-25 minutes only. Strain through fine mesh
fish_stock.storage_tips: Use within 2 days refrigerated or freeze for up to 2 months. Never simmer longer than 30 minutes or it turns bitter.

julienne.name: Julienne
julienne.practice_instructions: Cut vegetables into thin planks (3mm thick), stack planks, cut into uniform matchstick strips approximately 3mm x 3mm x 5cm. Practice with carrots first as they hold shape well. Focus on consistent thickness.

brunoise.name: Brunoise
brunoise.practice_instructions: First julienne the vegetable, then cut across the strips to create 3mm cubes. Requires very sharp knife and stable cutting board. Practice with firm vegetables like carrot and celery.

chiffonade.name: Chiffonade
chiffonade.practice_instructions: Stack herb or leafy green leaves, roll tightly into a cigar shape, slice across the roll to create fine ribbons. Use a sharp knife with gentle rocking motion. Works with basil, mint, and spinach.

tempering_spices.name: Tempering (Tadka/Tarka)
tempering_spices.practice_instructions: Heat oil or ghee until shimmering, add whole spices (mustard seeds, cumin seeds, curry leaves) in sequence from hardest to most delicate. Listen for crackling. Remove from heat when fragrant, about 30-60 seconds. Pour over finished dish.

deglazing.name: Deglazing
deglazing.practice_instructions: After searing meat, remove from pan. With pan still hot, add cold liquid (wine, stock, or vinegar). Scrape browned fond from bottom with wooden spoon. Reduce liquid by half for concentrated flavor. This forms the base of pan sauces.

emulsion.name: Emulsification
emulsion.practice_instructions: Slowly incorporate fat (oil or butter) into an acid or water base while whisking vigorously. Start with tiny amounts of fat. Temperature control is critical - too hot breaks the emulsion. Practice with vinaigrette before attempting hollandaise.

wok_hei.name: Wok Hei (Breath of the Wok)
wok_hei.practice_instructions: Heat wok until smoking on highest flame. Add oil, swirl to coat. Add ingredients in small batches. Toss food by pushing wok forward and flicking up. The momentary contact of food with the dry wok above the oil creates charred aroma. Requires gas burner.

velveting.name: Velveting
velveting.practice_instructions: Slice protein thinly against the grain. Marinate in mixture of egg white, cornstarch, rice wine, and salt for 15-30 minutes. Blanch briefly in 60-70C oil or water for 30 seconds. Drain and proceed to stir-fry. Results in silky, tender texture.

roux.name: Roux
roux.practice_instructions: Melt equal parts butter and flour by weight in a saucepan. Cook while stirring: white roux (2 minutes), blond roux (5 minutes), brown roux (15-20 minutes). Gradually whisk in liquid off heat to prevent lumps. Return to heat and simmer until desired thickness.

blanching.name: Blanching and Shocking
blanching.practice_instructions: Bring large pot of salted water to rapid boil. Prepare ice bath. Submerge vegetables for 30 seconds to 2 minutes depending on size. Immediately transfer to ice bath to stop cooking. Drain when fully cold. Preserves color, texture, and nutrients.

curry_paste_mortar.name: Curry Paste (Mortar and Pestle)
curry_paste_mortar.practice_instructions: Pound ingredients in order from hardest to softest: salt and hard spices first, then fibrous items (lemongrass, galangal), then softer aromatics (garlic, shallot, chilli). Pound and grind in circular motion until a smooth paste forms. Takes 15-20 minutes.

reduction.name: Reduction
reduction.practice_instructions: Simmer liquid uncovered over medium heat. Volume should decrease by half (demi) or more (glace). Stir occasionally and watch carefully as it thickens to prevent burning. Season after reducing as flavors concentrate. Use wide pan for faster evaporation.

chinese_five_spice.name: Chinese Five Spice
chinese_five_spice.flavor_description: Warm, aromatic, and slightly sweet with a numbing quality from Sichuan peppercorn. Balances all five flavor elements of Chinese cooking.

garam_masala.name: Garam Masala
garam_masala.flavor_description: Warm, complex, and aromatic. Added at the end of cooking to preserve volatile oils. Literally means 'warm spice blend'.

italian_soffritto.name: Soffritto
italian_soffritto.flavor_description: Sweet, aromatic, and savory base. The holy trinity of Italian cooking. Provides depth and body to sauces, soups, and braises.

thai_flavor_base.name: Thai Aromatic Base
thai_flavor_base.flavor_description: Bright, citrusy, and spicy with layers of heat and herbal complexity. The foundation of Thai curry pastes and soups.

japanese_tare.name: Japanese Tare (Sweet Soy Glaze)
japanese_tare.flavor_description: Sweet, salty, and deeply savory with pronounced umami. Balanced and glossy. The backbone of many Japanese glazes and sauces.

french_bouquet_garni.name: Bouquet Garni
french_bouquet_garni.flavor_description: Subtle, herbal, and aromatic. Provides a clean backbone of flavor without dominating. Removed before serving.

mirepoix.name: Mirepoix
mirepoix.flavor_description: The French equivalent of soffritto. Cut larger for stocks (rough chop) or finer for sauces. Ratio is 2:1:1 onion to carrot to celery.

cajun_spice.name: Cajun Spice Blend
cajun_spice.flavor_description: Bold, smoky, and moderately spicy with layers of heat from multiple pepper types. Earthy and aromatic.

dukkah.name: Dukkah
dukkah.flavor_description: Nutty, crunchy, and warmly spiced. A textural condiment that adds toasty complexity. Egyptian origin, popular in Australian cafes.

ras_el_hanout.name: Ras el Hanout
ras_el_hanout.flavor_description: Complex, floral, and warmly spiced. Literally 'head of the shop' meaning the best blend the spice merchant offers. No two versions are identical.
